sections.title: Voice and Tone
sections.description: NovaTel's brand voice is confident but not aggressive, direct but not pushy, human but not casual. We speak as a trusted advisor, not a salesperson.

rules.rule_text: NovaTel voice is confident but not aggressive, direct but not pushy, human but not casual. Write as if advising a smart friend.

examples.correct: Get the Galaxy S26 for $0/mo when you switch to our Unlimited Premium plan.
examples.violation: INSANE DEAL! FREE PHONE! YOU WON'T BELIEVE THIS!!!

rules.rule_text: Never use urgency-based pressure tactics including countdown timers, 'ACT NOW', 'LIMITED TIME ONLY' in all caps, 'HURRY', 'DON'T MISS OUT', or 'LAST CHANCE'.

examples.correct: This offer is available through June 30, 2026.
examples.violation: ⏰ HURRY! LIMITED TIME ONLY! ACT NOW BEFORE IT'S GONE!!!

rules.rule_text: Headlines and subject lines must not be in all-caps. Title Case or Sentence case only.

examples.correct: Switch to NovaTel and save on your family plan
examples.violation: SWITCH TO NOVATEL AND SAVE ON YOUR FAMILY PLAN

rules.rule_text: Avoid condescending language that implies the customer is uninformed. Do not use phrases like 'It's simple', 'Obviously', 'As everyone knows', or 'You probably don't know'.

examples.correct: Here's how bill credits work with your new phone.
examples.violation: It's simple — obviously, you get credits. As everyone knows, this is the best deal.

rules.rule_text: Use active voice. Avoid passive constructions where the actor is hidden (e.g., 'credits will be applied' → 'we apply credits').

examples.correct: We apply your trade-in credit as monthly bill credits over 36 months.
examples.violation: Credits will be applied to the account on a monthly basis for the duration of the installment agreement.

rules.rule_text: Humor is allowed but must not be sarcastic, dismissive of competitors by name, or make light of customer pain points (e.g., dropped calls, bill shock).

examples.correct: Your old carrier called. Just kidding — they dropped the call. 😏 (Okay, we won't actually say this.)
examples.violation: Tired of Verizon's garbage coverage? We get it.

sections.title: Approved Terminology
sections.description: Use only approved terms when describing NovaTel products, network capabilities, and offers. Deviations create regulatory risk.

rules.rule_text: '5G network' is the approved descriptor. '5G+' and 'Ultra Capacity 5G' require a parenthetical qualifier explaining the tier (e.g., '5G+ (our fastest, available in select metro areas)').

examples.correct: Stream on NovaTel's 5G network in over 300 cities.

rules.rule_text: 'fast' is allowed for general speed references. 'fastest' requires a cited independent source (Ookla, J.D. Power, or OpenSignal) published within the last 18 months, with test date and methodology noted.

examples.correct: NovaTel delivers fast 5G speeds across 300+ cities.
examples.violation: NovaTel has the fastest 5G network in America.

rules.rule_text: Use 'trade-in credit' when referring to device trade-in value. Never use 'cash back', 'rebate', 'refund', or 'money back' — these imply a monetary return the customer does not receive.

examples.correct: Get up to $1,000 in trade-in credit toward your new iPhone 17 Pro.
examples.violation: Get $1,000 cash back when you trade in your old phone!

rules.rule_text: Always use the exact, capitalized plan name: 'Unlimited Starter', 'Unlimited Extra', 'Unlimited Premium', 'Unlimited Ultimate', '5G Home'. Never abbreviate or create informal names.

examples.correct: Available with our Unlimited Premium plan.
examples.violation: Available with our premium unlimited plan.

rules.rule_text: Use 'AI-assisted' when describing NovaTel's AI capabilities. Do not use 'AI-powered', 'AI-driven', or 'intelligent' as standalone descriptors — these overstate current functionality.

examples.correct: AI-assisted network optimization helps maintain consistent speeds.
examples.violation: Our AI-powered network delivers intelligent performance.

rules.rule_text: Use 'works with' or 'connects to' for interoperability. Do not use 'integrates seamlessly', 'seamless integration', or 'plug-and-play'.

examples.correct: NovaTel 5G Home works with most Wi-Fi 6E routers.
examples.violation: NovaTel seamlessly integrates with your existing infrastructure.

rules.rule_text: 'bill credits' is the approved term for promotional device credits. Do not use 'discount', 'savings', or 'price reduction' when referring to installment credits.

examples.correct: You'll receive $30.56/mo in bill credits over 36 months.

rules.rule_text: When referencing network reliability or coverage, use 'broad coverage' or 'extensive coverage'. Do not use 'everywhere', 'nationwide coverage guaranteed', or '100% coverage'.

examples.correct: NovaTel offers broad 5G coverage across 300+ cities.
examples.violation: NovaTel 5G covers you everywhere you go.

sections.title: Prohibited Words and Phrases
sections.description: The following words and phrases are prohibited in all customer-facing NovaTel content unless specific exception conditions are met. Violations are classified by severity.

rules.condition: Prohibited without qualifier when the plan has deprioritization thresholds or speed reduction tiers. Must state threshold (e.g., 'Unlimited data — speeds may slow after 50GB during congestion').
Prohibited without immediately adjacent disclosure of ALL material conditions (plan requirement, trade-in, credit period, taxes due). '$0' follows the same rule.
Absolutely prohibited — implies no conditions exist, which is never true for carrier offers.
Prohibited without a cited independent source (Ookla, J.D. Power, OpenSignal, RootMetrics) published within 18 months.
Prohibited in all consumer-facing content due to legal liability. Use 'we stand behind' or 'we're confident' instead.
Prohibited as a verb in marketing copy. Use 'use', 'apply', or 'take advantage of'.
Prohibited — marketing filler. Use 'simply', 'easily', or remove entirely.
Prohibited without a cited third-party source and specific metric.
Prohibited — corporate jargon. Use 'help', 'enable', or 'give you'.
Prohibited — vague and overused. Be specific about what makes the network/product strong.
Prohibited — overused tech buzzword. Describe the actual innovation instead.
Prohibited — corporate jargon. Use 'combined benefit' or 'together' instead.
Prohibited as CTA text — violates accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1) and NovaTel CTA policy. Use descriptive CTA text.

sections.title: Channel Format Standards
sections.description: Each marketing channel has specific constraints. Content must be adapted to meet these requirements — not simply truncated.

rules.paragraph_style: Single block, no line breaks unless essential
must_include: Terms URL (novatel.com/terms or equivalent short URL)
rules.cta_format: Short URL only — no button text
rules.disclosure_rule: All mandatory disclosures must fit within the 160-character limit or the SMS must link to a terms page. Do NOT split promotional SMS into multiple messages to avoid disclosure.

rules.paragraph_style: 1-2 sentences per paragraph with white space between. No wall-of-text blocks exceeding 3 sentences.
rules.cta_format: Professional CTAs: 'See how it works', 'Compare plans', 'Book a demo'
rules.audience_note: LinkedIn skews professional — avoid consumer-casual tone. Lead with business outcome.
rules.disclosure_rule: Disclosures must be visible in the post body, not hidden below 'See More' fold.

rules.paragraph_style: 2-3 sentences per paragraph. Maximum 4 paragraphs in body. Subject line ≤ 60 characters.
rules.cta_format: 'Shop now', 'See plans', 'Switch today' — must be a button with tracked URL
rules.disclosure_rule: Full disclosure block at bottom of email. Key conditions (plan requirement, trade-in, credit period) must ALSO appear near the offer headline.

rules.paragraph_style: Short, scannable. Use line breaks generously. Emoji allowed but ≤ 3 per post.
rules.cta_format: 'Shop now', 'Learn more' (only on Meta platform CTA buttons, NOT in copy text)
rules.disclosure_rule: Material disclosures MUST be visible without requiring 'See More' click. On Instagram Stories, disclosures must be on the same frame as the claim.

rules.paragraph_style: 2-3 sentences per section. Use subheadings for scanability.
must_include: Plan comparison table if multiple plans referenced
rules.cta_format: Primary: 'Shop now' or 'Switch today'. Secondary: 'Compare plans'.
rules.disclosure_rule: Disclosures must appear in the same viewport as the triggering claim. Disclaimers below the fold are insufficient for above-the-fold claims.

rules.paragraph_style: AP Style. Inverted pyramid. 2-3 sentences per paragraph.
rules.cta_format: No CTA in body — press releases are informational. Include 'For more information, visit novatel.com' at end.
rules.disclosure_rule: Financial claims (revenue, subscriber counts) require 'as of [date]' qualifier.

sections.title: Audience Tone Calibration
sections.description: Adapt tone and content depth based on the target audience. Mismatched tone erodes trust and reduces conversion.

rules.guidance: Lead with business outcome and ROI. Minimize technical detail. Frame network as a reliability and productivity story. Use data points to support claims. No jargon, no casual language.

Lead with savings and simplicity. Avoid jargon entirely. Use conversational but not flippant tone. Explain trade-in credits in plain language. Assume the reader does not know what 'bill credits' means without context.
Lead with cost predictability and reliability. Small business owners care about 'will this work and what will it cost me.' Avoid enterprise-speak. Acknowledge they wear many hats.
example_phrases: one bill for all your lines

rules.guidance: Can use technical terminology (e.g., mid-band, mmWave, QoS, SASE). Lead with architecture, coverage specs, and API availability. Provide measurable claims with methodology.

sections.title: CTA Standards
sections.description: All calls-to-action must be clear, accessible, and traceable.

rules.rule_text: Approved CTAs for consumer content:
Approved CTAs for enterprise/B2B content:
Prohibited CTAs — these are either vague, inaccessible, or create false urgency:
prohibited_ctas: Buy now before it's too late
rules.reason: 'Click here' violates WCAG 2.1 accessibility guidelines. 'Learn more' is measurably less effective (per NovaTel A/B test data, Q1 2026). 'Act now' violates BRAND-102 urgency policy.

rules.rule_text: Every CTA must link to a tracked URL with UTM parameters (utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign at minimum). Untracked links are a HARD violation.

sections.title: Claim Standards
sections.description: Every factual claim in NovaTel marketing must be substantiated. Unsubstantiated claims create FTC/FCC risk and erode brand trust.

rules.rule_text: Quantitative claims (e.g., '40% faster', 'saves 3 hours/week', '99.9% uptime') require a cited source with methodology, sample size, date, and geographic scope.

examples.correct: NovaTel 5G delivered 37% faster median download speeds vs. the industry average in Q1 2026 (Ookla Speedtest Intelligence, Jan-Mar 2026, nationwide).
examples.violation: NovaTel is 40% faster than the competition.

rules.rule_text: Superlative claims ('fastest', 'most reliable', '#1', 'best-in-class') require an independent third-party source (Ookla, J.D. Power, OpenSignal, RootMetrics) published within 18 months.

examples.correct: Ranked #1 in network reliability by J.D. Power (U.S. Wireless Network Quality Study, Vol. 2, 2025).
examples.violation: NovaTel is the #1 network in America.

rules.rule_text: Customer testimonials and quotes require written approval from the customer and must include 'Results may vary' or equivalent disclaimer.

Comparative claims against competitors must not name the competitor directly in advertising. Use 'other major carriers' or 'the industry average'. Exception: press releases responding to specific competitive claims.

examples.correct: Switch from your current carrier and save up to 20%.
examples.violation: Switch from Verizon — NovaTel is cheaper and faster.

sections.title: Disclosure Requirements by Claim Type
sections.description: Specific claim types trigger mandatory disclosures. These are non-negotiable — failure to include them creates direct FTC/FCC liability.

mandatory_disclosures: Required plan name and monthly cost
Trade-in requirement and eligible device brands/conditions
Credit period (e.g., '36 monthly bill credits')
That credits stop if plan is changed or service cancelled
Applicable taxes due at purchase
rules.proximity_rule: Key conditions must appear within the same visual unit as the $0/Free claim. A footnote at bottom of page is insufficient.
rules.example_disclosure: $0/mo after bill credits when you trade in an eligible device in good condition on Unlimited Premium ($85.99/mo). Credits applied over 36 months; credits stop if you cancel or change plans. Tax on full device price due at purchase.

mandatory_disclosures: Test methodology (e.g., Ookla Speedtest Intelligence)
Date range of tests
Geographic scope (nationwide, specific markets)
Whether speeds cited are 'typical', 'median', or 'peak'
rules.proximity_rule: Citation must appear as a footnote on the same page/screen as the claim.
rules.example_disclosure: Based on Ookla Speedtest Intelligence data, median download speeds, Q1 2026, nationwide. Actual speeds vary by location, device, and network conditions.

mandatory_disclosures: Comparison baseline (which competitor, which plan tier)
Whether streaming services or bundled perks are factored into the comparison
AutoPay/paperless billing requirements
Whether taxes and fees are included in the advertised price
rules.proximity_rule: Comparison basis must be stated in the same sentence or immediately following the savings claim.
rules.example_disclosure: Save 20% vs. comparable unlimited plans from major carriers. Comparison based on single-line pricing with AutoPay. Includes value of bundled streaming ($15.99/mo). Taxes and fees extra.

mandatory_disclosures: New line activation required
Both devices must be financed
Second device value applied as monthly bill credits
Credit period and monthly credit amount
Remaining balance due if cancelled early
Activation fee ($35)
rules.proximity_rule: All conditions must be in the same ad unit. Cannot rely on 'See details' link alone.
rules.example_disclosure: BOGO: Buy iPhone 17, get iPhone 17 free. Req's 2 Unlimited Premium lines. Add a line req'd. 2nd device $0/mo via 36 monthly bill credits ($24.17/mo). Cancel/change plan = remaining balance due. $35 activation fee per line.

mandatory_disclosures: Deprioritization threshold (e.g., 'speeds may temporarily slow after 50GB during congestion')
Video streaming resolution limits (e.g., '480p on Starter, 4K on Premium')
Hotspot data limits and throttled speed after limit
rules.proximity_rule: Deprioritization disclosure must appear within the same content block as 'unlimited' claim.
rules.example_disclosure: Unlimited data on NovaTel's 5G network. On Unlimited Starter, speeds may temporarily slow during network congestion. Video streams at 480p. 5GB hotspot data included; slowed to 600Kbps after.
